title: Interview with Dr. Tyler Bland: Cinematic Clinical Narratives
bio: Clinical Assistant Professor, University of Idaho, WWAMI
description: In this inaugural episode, Leesa interviews Dr. Tyler Bland on two of his recent OAPF-funded articles. The articles discussed include [Enhancing Medical Student Engagement Through Cinematic Clinical Narratives: A Multimodal Generative AI-Based Teaching Method](https://doi.org/10.2196/63865) and [Antiparasitic Pharmacology Goes to the Movies: Leveraging Generative AI to Create Educational Short Films](https://doi.org/10.3390/ai6030060). Visit [U of I's Open Access Publishing Fund](https://www.lib.uidaho.edu/open/oapf/) for more information.
subject: artificial intelligence; education
source: Preferred Citation: "Interview with Dr. Tyler Bland: Cinematic Clinical Narratives", Open Invitation, University of Idaho Library

title: Interview with Dr. Yin Hong Cheah: Examining AI in Idaho Classrooms
bio: Assistant Professor of Instructional Technology, University of Idaho College of Education, Health, and Human Services
description: In this episode, Leesa and Marco interview Dr. Yin Hong Cheah about her experience as a first-time OAPF applicant and her research regarding AI adoption in Idaho classrooms. The article discussed is [Integrating generative artificial intelligence in K-12 education: Examining teachers’ preparedness, practices, and barriers](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.caeai.2025.100363). Visit [U of I's Open Access Publishing Fund](https://www.lib.uidaho.edu/open/oapf/) for more information.

title: Interview with Grace Peven: The Regenerative Power of Springs
bio: PhD Candidate, University of Idaho College of Natural Resources
description: In this episode, Leesa and Jeremy interview Grace Peven about her open publishing experience as a graduate student. The article discussed is [Montane springs provide regeneration refugia after high-severity wildfire](https://esajournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ecs2.70009). Visit [U of I's Open Access Publishing Fund](https://www.lib.uidaho.edu/open/oapf/) for more information.
